subject: English
skill: Main Idea, Key Details, and Text Structure

lesson.story_mission: Enter Information Architects Lab, where readers find the main idea, sort key details, notice structure, and use text features.
lesson.objective: I can determine main idea, summarize key details, identify text structure, and use text features.
lesson.concept_explanation: Informational readers ask what the whole passage is mostly about, which details matter most, and how the author organized ideas. Text structures such as sequence, cause and effect, compare and contrast, problem and solution, and description help readers summarize accurately.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may choose a topic instead of a full main idea, select tiny details, confuse structures, or name a text feature without explaining its purpose.
lesson.real_world_connection: Informational reading helps you learn from science articles, history pages, directions, websites, and research sources.
lesson.lesson_summary: Name the topic, state the main idea, choose key details, identify structure, and explain how text features help.
lesson.mini_lesson: First decide the topic. Then write a main idea sentence that covers the whole passage. Finally, use details, structure, and text features to support the summary.

story.text: Listen to this page. In this mission, you enter a Grade 4 reading studio for finding main idea, key details, text structure, and text features. You will learn why the skill matters, watch a model, practice with feedback, and then prove your strategy at checkpoint. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches finding main idea, key details, text structure, and text features. First, name the reading job. Next, use the visual model to notice important words and clues. Finally, answer in a way that matches the text instead of guessing.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page models the strategy. Listen for the teacher thinking aloud, because strong readers explain what they notice before they answer. Watch how the clue is connected to the correct answer.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo gives another guided example. Read the passage, point to the clue, and decide which answer is best supported. The goal is careful reading, not just speed.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Read the item, use the visual model, and press Read Question if you want the exact prompt read aloud. Press Listen when hearing the story, passage, or sentence will help. Choose or write an answer that fits the text.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. These questions ask you to apply finding main idea, key details, text structure, and text features with less help. Press Read Question for narration, and use Listen when audio helps you follow the reading. Slow down if two answers seem close.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show that you can use finding main idea, key details, text structure, and text features independently. Press Read Question if you want the prompt read aloud, and press Listen when audio will help you hear the passage. Answer carefully, then review your feedback.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your reading progress. You earned credit for using evidence, models, and careful thinking. Keep using this strategy whenever you meet a challenging text.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth and next steps. Review your accuracy, hints, and level progress. If one level needs more work, choose Practice This Skill and return to that level in the Skill Practice Center.
profile.label: Read This Page
